chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يُوجِبُ الْغُسْلَ عَلَى الرَّجُلِ وَ الْمَرْأَةِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 30 – What Obligates the washing upon the man and the woman

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/142)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/142)
text: إِذَا أَتَى الرَّجُلُ الْمَرْأَةَ فِي دُبُرِهَا فَلَمْ يُنْزِلْ فَلَا غُسْلَ عَلَيْهِمَا وَ إِنْ أَنْزَلَ فَعَلَيْهِ الْغُسْلُ وَ لَا غُسْلَ عَلَيْهَا .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Barqy,

(It has been narrated) raising it from Abu Abullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The man goes to the woman into her behind, but does not discharge, so there is no washing upon the two of them, and if he does discharges, so upon him is the washing, and there is no washing upon her’.
en.sarwar: 8. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Barqiy in a marfu‘ manner who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If a man allows intercourse to take place in a woman’s anus without discharge of semen, Ghusl (bath) is not necessary on neither one of them, but if he discharges, Ghusl (bath) is necessary on him but it is not necessary on the woman.’”
